_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of structure, approach, and strategy of exploitation tools (e.g., sniffers, keyloggers) and techniques (e.g., gaining backdoor access, collecting/exfiltrating data, conducting vulnerability analysis of other systems in the network).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data platform security controls, including network isolation, encryption at rest and in transit, identity and access management, and audit logging.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI DA domain. DA-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-001 (SP, cosine: 0.542), TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.534).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.4/10 (raw 0.542). Shared vocabulary: [access, data, knowledge, network]. DCWF-only terms [analysis, approach, backdoor, collecting, conducting, exfiltrating] vs WIDAI-only terms [audit, controls, encryption, identity, isolation, logging] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.861 (P=0.845, R=0.878). R exceeds P by 0.033 — the WIDAI KSA's 22 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 29 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (100.0%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of structure, approach, and strategy of exploitation tools (e.g., snif" and "Knowledge of data platform security controls, including network isolation, encry". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.632 > STS 0.542 (gap +0.090). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [access, data, knowledge, network] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.5%. Shared tokens: [access, data, knowledge, network]. DCWF-only: [analysis, approach, backdoor, collecting, conducting, exfiltrating]. WIDAI-only: [audit, controls, encryption, identity, isolation, logging].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 100.0% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.542 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
STS 0.542 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
